description: The My4Blocks Cognitive Distortion Detection Library. Built upon Dr. Parr's Seven Irrational Beliefs — the primary thinking patterns that trigger emotional suffering in the Four Blocks framework. Used for RAG routing, distortion identification, and user awareness. Build this library last — it requires all other libraries to be in place first.
core_reference: Dr. Parr's Seven Irrational Beliefs — the foundational cognitive distortions underlying all four emotional blocks.
source: NotebookLM cross-reference against My4Blocks methodology backbone

distortions.definition: Believing that someone or something outside of the self is responsible for one's internal emotional state. Assigning cause-and-effect power to external events rather than accepting 100% responsibility for internal reactions.
distortions.irrational_core: A-to-C thinking — the belief that the Activating Event directly causes the emotional Consequence, bypassing the Belief entirely.
example_thoughts: He made me so angry when he cut me off in traffic.
This slow computer is really ticking me off.
She ruined my whole day with that comment.
distortions.rational_replacement: Nothing and no one else can cause my thoughts and feelings. The event happened, and I am the one creating my internal response to it. I am 100% responsible for my emotional reaction.
distortions.awareness_prompt: You are describing the event as the cause of your feeling. The event is the A — your belief about it is the B — your emotion is the C. What are you telling yourself about what happened?

distortions.definition: Exaggerating an unpleasant situation until it is perceived as 100% negative, catastrophic, or off the scale of human suffering. Treating a bad situation as the worst possible outcome.
distortions.irrational_core: The belief that a situation is not merely bad but catastrophically, unbearably, irredeemably awful — treating inconvenience as catastrophe.
example_thoughts: It would be horrible if my partner didn't love me anymore.
Losing this job would be a total disaster.
If I fail this exam my life is over.
distortions.rational_replacement: This situation is very unpleasant and inconvenient, but it is not 100% awful. On a scale of 0 to 100%, it could always be worse. I can stand unpleasant things.
distortions.awareness_prompt: You are rating this situation as a catastrophe. How awful is it really on a scale of 0 to 100%? What would 100% awful actually look like? Is this situation truly there?

distortions.distortion_name: I Can't Stand It — ICSI — Low Frustration Tolerance
distortions.definition: The false belief that one cannot survive, endure, or function under current unpleasant conditions. Claiming that a situation is unendurable when the evidence — being alive and present — proves otherwise.
distortions.irrational_core: Treating temporary discomfort as permanent unendurability. The paradox is that the user is always standing whatever they claim they cannot stand.
example_thoughts: I just can't handle this pressure.
I can't take it anymore — something is going to snap.
I can't stand being treated this way.
distortions.rational_replacement: I have stood every single thing that has ever happened to me in my life up until this second. I am standing this right now and I will live through it.
distortions.awareness_prompt: You said you cannot stand this. But you are standing it right now — which is proof that you can. What would it look like to simply accept the discomfort rather than fight it?

distortions.distortion_name: Shoulds Musts and Demands — SMDs — Demandingness
distortions.definition: Turning a rational preference into a rigid, absolutistic demand on the self, others, or the world. Arguing with reality as it is rather than accepting it and working from acceptance.
distortions.irrational_core: The belief that reality must conform to personal preferences — that there is a law of the universe requiring people and circumstances to be different than they are.
example_thoughts: I must perform perfectly at work.
People should be better drivers.
My partner must treat me with more respect.
I shouldn't have made that mistake.
distortions.rational_replacement: Everything that happens today should happen because all trillions of conditions for it to occur were met. I would prefer things to be different, but they are as they are. I replace the demand with a preference.
distortions.awareness_prompt: You are using a Should or a Must. Is that a preference or a demand? What would happen to your emotional state if you changed 'they must' to 'I would strongly prefer they'?

distortions.distortion_name: Rating — Global Self and Other Condemnation
distortions.definition: Assigning a global label of worth or worthlessness to oneself or another person based on a single behavior, mistake, or trait. Treating a complex, ever-changing human being as definable by one action.
distortions.irrational_core: The BS Equation — Behavior equals Self (B = S). The belief that a person's worth can be rated at all — up or down — based on performance or behavior.
example_thoughts: I'm a total failure because my marriage ended.
He is a worthless person for breaking that promise.
I'm stupid for making that mistake.
She is a bad person.
distortions.rational_replacement: Behavior equals Behavior (B=B) but Behavior does not equal the Self (B does not equal S). I accept myself and others as Fallible Human Beings regardless of our traits or acts.
distortions.awareness_prompt: You are rating your entire Self based on one behavior. Can a complex human being — an ever-changing system of trillions of cells and experiences — be reduced to one label? What would Unconditional Self-Acceptance look like here?

distortions.definition: Using extreme terms to describe conditions as permanent or unchanging. Viewing the world in black-and-white extremes — always, never, everything, nothing — that deny the ever-changing nature of reality.
distortions.irrational_core: Hopelessness (H1) — the belief that current negative conditions are permanent and unchangeable. Denying the ever-changing nature of reality.
example_thoughts: I'll always be alone.
Nothing ever works out for me.
I'll never be happy.
Everything always goes wrong.
detection_language: it will always be this way
distortions.rational_replacement: Reality is an ever-changing system and nothing is permanent. This is my experience in this moment, but I cannot predict that it will always be this way. Staying Now.
distortions.awareness_prompt: You used an absolute — always or never. Is that literally true? Has there ever been a single exception? Reality is an ever-changing system. What if this moment is just this moment — not forever?

distortions.definition: The egocentric belief that one is uniquely deserving of respect, convenience, fair treatment, or special consideration simply because of who they are. Demanding the universe operate according to personal preferences.
distortions.irrational_core: Egocentric Thinking (ET) — the belief that the universe is arranged around the self and must deliver preferred conditions as a matter of right.
example_thoughts: I am entitled to better treatment than this.
I deserve respect from my coworkers.
After everything I've done, I deserve more.
People should know better than to treat me this way.
detection_language: I shouldn't have to deal with this
distortions.rational_replacement: The universe does not give a damn about me or what happens to me. I would prefer to be treated well, but I am not entitled to it as a law of nature. I replace entitlement with preference.
distortions.awareness_prompt: You believe you are entitled to this treatment. What is the law of the universe that guarantees you this? Could you hold this as a strong preference rather than a right — and notice how your emotional state shifts?

routing_logic.detection_method: Match user language against detection_language arrays for each distortion. Multiple distortions may be active simultaneously.
routing_logic.primary_routing: Identify distortion first → confirm formula reference → deliver rational replacement → apply awareness prompt if user is receptive.
routing_logic.multi_distortion_protocol: When multiple distortions are detected, address the most emotionally activated one first. Sequence: ICSI → Awfulizing → SMDs → Rating → Absolutistic → Entitlement → It Statements.
routing_logic.formula_return_rule: Every distortion detection must route back to the applicable Four Blocks formula. Detection without formula application is incomplete.

implementation_notes.build_order: This library is built last. All scenario libraries, trigger libraries, relationship libraries, and the emotional pattern memory library must be in place before this library is ingested.
implementation_notes.pipeline_position: Runs as secondary detection layer after scenario matching. Adds distortion-level specificity to the emotional pattern already identified.
implementation_notes.source_tool: NotebookLM — cross-referenced against My4Blocks methodology backbone
